macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: US Stock Futures Plunge Amid Iran War and Oil Spike
items.summary: S&P futures down 1.3-1.8%, Dow futures off 820-977 points, Nasdaq futures down 2% as markets react to fourth day of war against Iran, crude oil spiking 9%, and inflation concerns reducing expectations for Fed rate cuts.

items.title: Panic Hits Korean Stocks and Global Markets on Mideast Conflict
items.summary: Panic sweeps Korean stocks with world markets down nearly 3% premarket; European indices drop over three standard deviations due to energy disruptions and inflation fears from Mideast tensions.

items.title: Wall Street Selloff Driven by Oil Shock in Strait of Hormuz Standoff
items.summary: Wall Street rattled by surging oil prices as Iran tensions intensify in the Strait of Hormuz, prompting a broad market decline across all sectors.

items.title: Memory Chip Stocks Tumble Premarket on Profit Taking
items.summary: SanDisk and Micron down 6% premarket after rapid run-ups, contributing to broader tech sector weakness amid geopolitical volatility.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Tumbles Below $70,000 Amid Global Risk-Off Sentiment
items.summary: Bitcoin briefly rallied above $70,000 but fell as investors fled riskier assets due to Strait of Hormuz closure concerns and South Korean market tumble. Ether, Solana, and XRP also declined 2-2.6%, while stocks like MicroStrategy and Riot Platforms dropped over 2%.

items.title: Circle Stock Surges Up to 24% as Stablecoin Market Breaks $300 Billion
items.summary: Circle's stock popped nearly 7% to 24% amid broader crypto market declines, with mainstream stablecoins performing well and total stablecoin market cap surpassing $300 billion. Analysts raised price targets on Circle despite red markets elsewhere.

items.title: Strait of Hormuz Closure Reports Trigger Crypto and Global Market Sell-Off
items.summary: Reported Strait of Hormuz closure amid Iran-Israel tensions drove oil higher, strengthened the US dollar, and caused risk-off moves in crypto and stocks, halting Bitcoin's rally. South Korean and Japanese markets' declines amplified the Bitcoin pullback.

items.title: Trump-Affiliated American Bitcoin Company Expands Mining Capacity by 12%
items.summary: The American Bitcoin Company, linked to Trump, is doubling down on a pure Bitcoin mining and hoarding strategy by expanding mining capacity around 12%. This move comes amid broader market volatility.

items.title: US Clarity Act Negotiations May Restrict Stablecoin Yield Distribution
items.summary: Discussions on the US Clarity Act are nearing agreement, but it may prevent Circle from distributing yield on stablecoins, raising questions about its overall benefits for the industry.

items.title: South Korea Addresses Crypto Asset Vulnerabilities with Tighter Regulations
items.summary: South Korea is urgently patching regulatory shortcomings after exposing asset vulnerabilities, amid tumbling local markets impacting global crypto sentiment.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: GSMA Launches Open Telco AI Initiative with AT&T and AMD as Founding Partners
items.summary: The GSMA has launched an industry initiative to develop open, telco-specific AI models, addressing limitations of current models in handling telco tasks. Founding partners include AT&T and AMD.

items.title: JetStream Secures $34M Seed Funding for AI Governance Platform
items.summary: Startup JetStream raised $34 million in seed funding to address enterprise AI governance challenges. The company introduced blueprint-based controls for managing AI deployments.

items.title: Intel Discusses AI Innovations in 5G and Path to 6G
items.summary: Intel executives outlined advancements in AI integration with 5G across RAN, Core, and Edge, while discussing the roadmap to 6G. The discussion highlights leadership in telecom AI infrastructure.

items.title: AI Infrastructure Market Report Highlights Advances in GPUs, TPUs, and Edge AI
items.summary: A new market research report details opportunities in specialized hardware like GPUs, TPUs, and ASICs for deep learning, alongside the rise of edge AI enabled by efficient accelerators and 5G.

items.title: 5 Key AI Automation Trends Shaping Business for Next 5 Years
items.summary: Major trends include autonomous decision-making systems, hyperautomation, AI-augmented workforces, intelligent customer experiences, and AI-driven risk management. These shifts emphasize real-time AI execution and personalization.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Seven Games Coming to Xbox Game Pass in March, Including Cyberpunk 2077 and Planet of Lana 2
items.summary: Microsoft announced seven new titles joining Xbox Game Pass from March 4-17, 2026, with major additions including Cyberpunk 2077, EA Sports F1 25, and the day-one launch of Planet of Lana 2. Other notable releases include Hollow Knight: Silksong and Construction Simulator.

items.title: Nintendo Indie World Showcase Reveals Multiple Switch 2 Titles and Release Dates
items.summary: Nintendo's March 3 Indie World showcase announced numerous indie games for Nintendo Switch and Switch 2, including Rotwood (console exclusive), Minishoot' Adventures, and Heave Ho 2, with releases ranging from today through summer 2026.

items.title: The Division Resurgence Mobile Game Launches March 31 on iOS and Android
items.summary: Ubisoft is releasing The Division Resurgence, a free-to-play mobile MMO that was first announced in 2021, on March 31 for both iOS and Android. The game features cross-progression rewards with The Division 2 and celebrates the franchise's 10th anniversary.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Apple Launches New MacBook Air and Pro with M5 Chips
items.summary: Apple unveiled a new MacBook Air with M5 chip, MacBook Pro models with M5 Pro and M5 Max processors, and refreshed Studio Displays as part of its staggered March event rollout starting March 2. These updates focus on performance improvements without major design changes.

items.title: Apple Reveals iPhone 17e and M4 iPad Air
items.summary: Apple announced the affordable iPhone 17e with A19 chip and Ceramic Shield 2 display, alongside the M4 iPad Air featuring up to 30% faster performance, Wi-Fi 7, and Bluetooth 6. More launches like low-cost MacBook and iPad 12 are expected through March 4.

items.title: NVIDIA Invests $4B in Photonics Suppliers for Data Centers
items.summary: NVIDIA announced $2 billion investments each in Lumentum and Coherent, plus multiyear commitments, to secure photonics components for scaling AI data center networking and performance. This move aims to control the supply chain for optical interconnects.

items.title: Broadcom Q1 FY2026 Earnings Preview Highlights AI Revenue Growth
items.summary: Markets expect Broadcom's AI and ASIC revenue to hit $8.2 billion, over 100% year-on-year growth, driven by hyperscaler demand for chips and networking in data centers. Results will test sustainable growth amid cost pressures.

items.title: Global Smartphone Market Faces Record Decline in 2026
items.summary: The smartphone market is set for its biggest-ever shipment decline in 2026 due to falling memory prices, reaching the lowest levels in over a decade.
